workstreams_completed.name: Admin API Routes — pit-assignment-state GET + slots/[slotId]/assign POST/DELETE
workstreams_completed.gate_evidence: npm run lint EXIT=0 (services/floor-layout/ + app/api/v1/floor-layouts/); npx eslint --no-warn-ignored on the 2 new route files EXIT=0; npm run type-check EXIT=0. ADR-024 INV-8 preserved (route handlers pass only tableId/slotId to service; no casino_id or actor_id). Admin gate inline in all three handlers per EXEC §WS3 L884-902 — FORBIDDEN_ADMIN_REQUIRED → 403 via DomainError. Idempotency-Key required on POST/DELETE only.

deviations.rationale: All existing app/api/v1/floor-layouts/ routes use 'floor-layout' (kebab-case) — verified via grep. EXEC-SPEC §WS3 used 'floor_layout' (snake) but project convention for the audit-domain string is kebab. Aligning to existing convention is lower-risk than introducing inconsistency in audit log domain values.

deviations.exec_spec_value: GET admin-gate spelled out in §WS3 L884 ('All three handlers') but the dispatch summary noted only the mutation gates
deviations.implemented_value: Added inline admin check on GET as well, per EXEC §WS3 L884-902 'All three handlers' clause + acceptance criterion 'Admin-role rejection returns 403 with envelope { code: FORBIDDEN_ADMIN_REQUIRED }' (L908-909). Panel is admin-only, so read-side gate matches spec intent.
deviations.rationale: Spec authority stronger than dispatch summary; covered all three handlers consistently.

scope_expansion_flags.rationale: 8 additive FloorLayoutErrorCode entries (NO_ACTIVE_LAYOUT, SLOT_NOT_FOUND, SLOT_NOT_ACTIVE, SLOT_HAS_NO_PIT, SLOT_OCCUPIED, PIT_NOT_FOUND, CROSS_CASINO_FORBIDDEN, FORBIDDEN_ADMIN_REQUIRED) required to surface RPC exceptions as typed DomainError per WS2 acceptance criterion. workstreams_completed.name: Integration Tests — RPC + Service + RLS Boundary
workstreams_completed.gate_evidence: RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true against local Supabase: 35/35 tests passing (22 RPC + 13 service). Run-time ~2s for full suite. type-check exit 0, lint exit 0. All EXEC §WS5 groups (a)-(m) green including: admin-role gate, non-admin rejection, cross-casino byte-identical mirror, inactive-slot rejection, occupied-target rejection, idempotent clear, atomic move, partial unique index (23505 on bypass), DEC-001 mirror verification (assign→label, clear→NULL, cross-casino untouched), audit_log emission, transactional rollback via pg-client trigger, concurrent-move consistency, DEC-003-R8 drift containment.
implementation_notes: Added `pg` (already a dependency) direct-SQL path for group (k) rollback test — PostgREST can't install BEFORE INSERT triggers, so uses postgresql://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:54322/postgres. Graceful skip w/ warning if connection fails.

Group (l) rewritten: original 'one wins, one fails' premise was incorrect (FOR UPDATE lock on floor_layout_activation serializes concurrent moves). Now tests actual guarantee: final-state consistency (exactly one slot holds table, gaming_table.pit mirrors winning slot's pit label).
Fixture setup time ~60s. beforeEach resets floor_table_slot.preferred_table_id + gaming_table.pit + audit_log between tests (cleanAssignmentState helper).
Mode C JWT auth: 5 authenticated clients (adminA, adminA2, pitBossA, dealerA, adminB); service-role setupClient for fixture CRUD.

outputs: components/admin/pit-configuration-panel.tsx (single 'use client', brutalist DNA, per-pit slot list with Select/Clear, unassigned-tables pool, skeleton, admin-only controls, useTransition, error banner)
hooks/floor-layout/use-pit-assignment-mutations.ts (useAssignOrMoveTableToSlot + useClearSlotAssignment, auto idempotency-key via crypto.randomUUID, invalidate pitAssignmentState on success)
hooks/floor-layout/index.ts (barrel: re-exports three hooks + variable types)

app/(dashboard)/admin/settings/operations/page.tsx (modified: renders ShiftSettingsForm + PitConfigurationPanel inside single composed SettingsContentSection — EXEC §WS4 allows single-composed option. No new top-level route.)
services/floor-layout/http.ts (cleanup: removed unused generateIdempotencyKey — TS6133 dead code flagged pre-WS4)
workstreams_completed.gate_evidence: npm run type-check EXIT=0 (final log /tmp/ws4-final.log); npm run lint EXIT=0 scoped to components/admin/pit-configuration-panel.tsx + hooks/floor-layout/ + app/(dashboard)/admin/settings/operations/page.tsx + services/floor-layout/http.ts. React 19 patterns: useTransition for pending, no useEffect sync patterns, no manual loading states. Admin-only affordance verified: Select/Clear controls rendered only when staffRole === 'admin' (server still rejects with FORBIDDEN_ADMIN_REQUIRED as defence-in-depth). No new top-level route introduced.

deviations.exec_spec_value: separate SettingsContentSection wrappers OR a single composed section (EXEC §WS4 L940-941 allows either)
deviations.implemented_value: Single composed SettingsContentSection with inline sub-section header (LayoutGrid icon + monospace 'Pit Configuration' title + Separator)
deviations.rationale: SettingsContentSection has flex-1 intended for one-per-page host layout. Two stacked SettingsContentSections caused height-split under the settings/layout.tsx flex-1 overflow-y-hidden container. Single composed section is the spec's explicit allowed alternative and matches the visual flow of the page (Casino Operations umbrella covers both shift settings and pit config).

design_checklist_results.notes: Optimistic updates intentionally skipped — admin mutations are low-frequency and TanStack invalidation is sufficient. Out of WS4 scope.

validation_warnings: SCHEMA GATE: Spec has schema changes but no explicit schema-verification gate. Consider adding 'npm test schema-verification' per QA standards.

infrastructure_findings.summary: Both .env and .env.local set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL to the REMOTE Supabase (Supabase Cloud at vaicxfihdldgepzryhpd.supabase.co). Commented-out local URL is in .env. Integration tests must explicitly override env vars to hit local docker Supabase.
infrastructure_findings.workaround: Run integration tests with explicit local env: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:54321 NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<LOCAL_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY> SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=<LOCAL_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY> RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true npx jest ...
infrastructure_findings.recommended_fix: Add a test:integration:local npm script that exports these, OR uncomment the local URL in .env and document dev precedence. For DevOps to triage — not blocking WS3.
infrastructure_findings.discovered_during: WS5 gate execution — PGRST202 error on new RPCs led to investigation that revealed remote vs local URL mismatch.

infrastructure_findings.summary: services/floor-layout/http.ts generateIdempotencyKey function (line 49) is never called — pre-existing dead code, unrelated to PRD-067 but flagged by TS6133 after WS2 edits.
infrastructure_findings.recommended_fix: Remove in a follow-up cleanup commit.

adversarial_review.tier_override_reason: User override: actual risk surface is narrow (ADR-024 compliance on 2 new RPCs + RULE-3 mechanism choice); R1+R2 cover the load-bearing risk.

attempt_history.notes: R1=Ship-with-gates (clerical-only, applied inline); R2=Ship. All 3 P0s + 9 P1s from attempt 1 resolved.

User-authored spec tightening applied: (1) demoted admin-only language in R6 and ADR-030 posture; (2) added normative Mirror Authority Rule (post-PRD-067 gaming_table.pit = compatibility-derived only); (3) tightened R6 to required hardening follow-up (R6-FU), no longer indefinite deferral; (4) clarified WS3 DELETE handler is co-located with POST in single route file; (5) de-inflated DEC-003 language — two layers are operational containment of the PRD-067 mutation path, not activation-lifecycle fixes.
